cards.back: The presence of pre-existing prejudices or discriminatory tendencies embedded in the data used to train an algorithm, leading to unfair outcomes
The lack of transparency and explainability in algorithmic decision-making processes, making it difficult to identify potential biases or errors
The process of designing algorithms that align with human values and moral principles, ensuring that AI systems serve the greater good
The ability of algorithms to provide clear and understandable explanations for their decisions, enabling trust and accountability
The principle of ensuring that algorithms treat individuals fairly and without bias, regardless of their background, demographic, or other factors
